issue_title: Deprecation of https-trust-store-* weakens X509 browser authentication
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

Deprecation of https-trust-store-file-* options prevents specifying a dedicated trust store for X509 authentication.
So, any client cert emitted by extra CAs (such as Digicert) are trusted for client auth, which disables most of control on client certification validation.

### Version

25.0.6

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Trusted CAs for X509 browser authentication should be ONLY the ones specified in a dedicated trust store.

### Actual behavior

Trusted CAs are merged with the default Java trust store ([Configuring the System Truststore](https://www.keycloak.org/server/keycloak-truststore)). So, for X509 browser authentication, all certificates emitted by the CAs in the default Java trust store are trusted !

### How to Reproduce?

#### Generate a TLS certificate for the server
```
mkdir ssl
keytool -genkeypair -storepass password -storetype PKCS12 -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -dname "CN=localhost" -alias server -ext "SAN:c=DNS:localhost,IP:127.0.0.1" -keystore ssl/server.keystore
```

#### Start the server, enabling X509 client auth
`docker run --rm -p 8443:8443 -v $PWD/ssl:/opt/keycloak/conf/ssl -e KC_LOG_LEVEL=info quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:25.0.6 --verbose start --https-key-store-file=/opt/keycloak/conf/ssl/server.keystore --https-client-auth=request --hostname=localhost`
#### View trusted CAs
`openssl s_client -connect localhost:8443 -prexit`

...
Acceptable client certificate CA names
C = US, ST = Illinois, L = Chicago, O = "Trustwave Holdings, Inc.", CN = Trustwave Global ECC P256 Certification Authority
C = US, O = Certainly, CN = Certainly Root E1
C = US, O = SecureTrust Corporation, CN = SecureTrust CA
C = US, O = "Entrust, Inc.", OU = See www.entrust.net/legal-terms, OU = "(c) 2012 Entrust, Inc. - for authorized use only", CN = Entrust Root Certification Authority - EC1
C = US, O = DigiCert Inc, OU = www.digicert.com, CN = DigiCert Global Root CA
...

### Anything else?

Related issue : [ Truststore consolidation broke keycloak fips mode #28526 ](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/28526)
commit_message:
Undeprecate `https-trust-store-*` options and enhance mTLS docs

Closes #33172

Signed-off-by: Václav Muzikář <vmuzikar@redhat.com>

issue_title: Any one Client role mapping to user/group generating two events on admin events tab.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

With enabling admin event configuration. When we do any role mapping with client role. We are seeing two events.

1. with REALM_ROLE_MAPPING as resource_type
2. with CLIENT_ROLE_MAPPING as resource_type


Here 2nd event is perfect representation of what one would like to see as audit trail while 1st event with REALM_ROLE_MAPPING and representation json as NULL is bit confusing for the user and wrong as audit trail.

I have recorded video and attached for more clarity.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/95a9617d-374a-4de9-b553-8efa5e5a96e8

25.0.6(latest)

- [ ] The issue is a regression

admin event tab should only have one event with CLIENT_ROLE_MAPPING as resource_type.

### Actual behavior

Admin even tab shows two events. with resource_type CLIENT_ROLE_MAPPING  and REALM_ROLE_MAPPING

### How to Reproduce?

1. Set the admin event enabled configuration.
2. Assign any client role to user.
3. Check the admin event tab
4. you will see 2 new events get created on one client role assignment activity.

commit_message:
Persist admin event only when roles is non-empty

Currently, an adminEvent is created regardless of if the roles passed to the
role-mapping API is empty. The event should only be created when the list
`roles` is non-empty.

Closes #33195

issue_body:

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When exporting a realm with orgs, the organization ids are not exported, and are re-generated when imported back. This prevents the ids from being mapped into access tokens, for example.

See discussion https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/32364 for more details on why this is problematic

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Organization ids should be preserved on export/import so that any tokens referencing the org by id would still be valid

### Actual behavior

The ids are regenerated once the realm with the orgs is re-imported.

### How to Reproduce?

Export a realm with organizations, then re-import it. Check that the org ids have changed.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: 400 error logged as 500
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

When a request with missing `code`, `clientId`, or `tabId` is sent to `GET .../{provider_alias}/login`, Keycloak logs two error messages. Moreover, the second logged message suggests that a 500 internal server error has occurred, which isn't the case.
```
Sep 19, 2024 1:40:53 PM org.keycloak.services.resources.IdentityBrokerService fireErrorEvent
ERROR: invalidRequestMessage
Sep 19, 2024 1:40:53 PM org.keycloak.services.resources.IdentityBrokerService fireErrorEvent
ERROR: unexpectedErrorHandlingRequestMessage: org.keycloak.services.ErrorPageException: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error
```

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Only a 400 error should be logged.

### Actual behavior

Two errors are logged, with one misleadingly indicating a 500 internal server error.

### How to Reproduce?

Send a request to `GET .../{provider_alias}/login` without `code`.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix errors when code, clientId, or tabId are null

Calling parseSessionCode inside the try-catch would result in
ErrorPageException thrown by redirectToErrorPage being caught and
re-reported, resulting in one log entry with `invalidRequestMessage`
and another one with `unexpectedErrorHandlingRequestMessage`.

Additionally, one of ErrorPageException constructors didn't pass the
status to super(), resulting in the logger error message being
"HTTP 500 Internal Server Error" even though the status was actually
something else, like 400. I noticed that ErrorPageException can be
simplified by just passing the response to super(), which is one way of
fixing the problem.

Closes #33232

Signed-off-by: Krzysztof Szafrański <k.p.szafranski@gmail.com>

issue_title: [Identity Providers] remove sorting in the admin console IDP lists
issue_body:
### Description

The identity provider pages in the admin console (both the main idp page and the one inside organizations) sort the providers by alias. The main IDP page shouldn't have to sort by alias because all results are already fetched from the DB ordered by alias.

For organizations, the cache that was introduced in the `IdentityProviderStorageProvider.getByOrganization` method is storing the ids of the IDPs in a set, which breaks the ordering from the DB and forces the UI to sort, which won't work well with pagination.

This task aims to fix the order of the organization IDPs returned (either by storing them in a List to preserve the DB order or by calling `sort` on the returning stream) and then remove the unnecessary sorting from the UI pages.

Ensure IDPs returned from infinispan provider are ordered by alias

Closes #33243

issue_title: Migrating to a FIPS environment disallows all users from logging in
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

I have been following the guidance set out in https://www.keycloak.org/server/fips to move my non-FIPS Keycloak environment to a FIPS-enabled environment.
I am currently testing in a development environment where I have an existing database of users.

Although the documentation has got me as far as getting my FIPS-enabled instance up and running, I can not log in with any existing users (with passwords less than and greater than 14 characters). I have tried in both strict and non-strict mode.
My version of Keycloak is 25.0.5

I suspect that the passwords are hashing differently and so when I turn on FIPS, the hashes do not match and a user password is deemed as incorrect.

I am able to workaround the issue by first deploying KC in non-fips mode and setting `--spi-password-hashing-provider-default` to pbkdf2-sha512. I then log in with my test users (should mean their password is re-hashed into the algorithm that FIPS uses) and I can then re-deploy the KC service with FIPS enabled and log into users successfully.
I should also say that when strict mode is on, although I pass `--spi-password-hashing-pbkdf2-sha512-max-padding-length=14`, I can still only log in with users whose passwords are longer than 14 chars.


### Version

25.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I expect that when I turn on FIPS and re-deploy my KC service, that users should be able to log in before their passwords are re-hashed.

### Actual behavior

When I turn on FIPS mode and try to log in with existing users (with passwords of less than and greater than 14 characters), I receive the error that the username or password is incorrect

### How to Reproduce?

Run a Keycloak environment with version 25.0.5 and create users with passwords of varying lengths.
Re-deploy Keycloak environment with FIPS now turned on.
Try to log in with existing users.


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: XA Transaction recovery support is enabled even thoug transaction-xa-enabled is false
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Trying to run Keycloak 25 in Kubernetes with read-only root filesystem will fail with
```
com.arjuna.ats.arjuna.exceptions.ObjectStoreException: ARJUNA012225: FileSystemStore::setupStore - cannot access root of object store: /opt/keycloak/bin/../data/transaction-logs/ShadowNoFileLockStore/defaultStore/
```

From [upgrading guide](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/25.0.0/upgrading/#xa-transaction-changes)
> The option transaction-xa-enabled will default to false
> XA Transaction recovery support is enabled by default if transaction-xa-enabled is true

I came to the conclusion, that recovery should be disabled by default, because XA is disabled. But it is not the case as we can see from log.

### Version

25.0.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Start successfully

### Actual behavior

Fails to start

### How to Reproduce?

1. Build custom optimized image
```
FROM quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:${VERSION}

ENV KC_CACHE=local
ENV KC_DB=postgres
ENV KC_HEALTH_ENABLED=true
ENV KC_METRICS_ENABLED=true

RUN /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh build
```
2. Try to run
```
services:
  keycloak:
    image: registry/keycloak:std-pg-25.0.6
    container_name: keycloak
    command: start
    environment:
      KC_DB: postgres
      KC_DB_URL: jdbc:postgresql://postgres:5432/keycloak
      KC_DB_USERNAME: keycloak
      KC_DB_PASSWORD: password
      KC_HOSTNAME: localhost
      KC_HTTP_ENABLED: true
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN: admin
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD: admin
    depends_on:
      - postgres
    ports:
      - 8080:8080
    volumes:
      - keycloak_tmp:/tmp
    read_only: true

  postgres:
    image: postgres:16
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: keycloak
      POSTGRES_USER: keycloak
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password

volumes:
  postgres_data:
  keycloak_tmp:
```
3. Get error

```
keycloak    | 2024-09-26 13:12:35,168 WARN  [com.arjuna.ats.common] (main) ARJUNA048006: cannot create new instance of com.arjuna.ats.internal.arjuna.objectstore.ShadowNoFileLockStore: java.lang.reflect.InvocationTargetException
keycloak    | Caused by: com.arjuna.ats.arjuna.exceptions.ObjectStoreException: ARJUNA012225: FileSystemStore::setupStore - cannot access root of object store: /opt/keycloak/bin/../data/transaction-logs/ShadowNoFileLockStore/defaultStore/
keycloak    | 2024-09-26 13:12:35,175 INFO  [com.arjuna.ats.jbossatx] (main) ARJUNA032014: Stopping transaction recovery manager
```

### Anything else?

Works as expected if build/run with `QUARKUS_TRANSACTION_MANAGER_ENABLE_RECOVERY: false`.
There is also a hack to mount `emptyDir` volume to `/opt/keycloak/data`.
commit_message:
fix: correcting the recovery upgrade note (#33356)

closes: #33307

issue_title: External token (not issued by Keycloak) cannot be validated in token exchange flow in case user info check is disabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

When exchanging an access token issued by an external provider (e.g. Microsoft configured as OpenID Connect v1.0) and user info check disabled, keycloak is returning error `invalid_token` because the token doesn't contain claim `typ`. The 
`typ` claim is optional and some providers (e.g. Microsoft) are not setting it. The regression was introduced with https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/28866

Suggested change is to validate the `typ` claim only if it is available and make it required only in `KeycloakOIDCIdentityProvider` 

in `OIDCIdentityProvider` 
```
protected boolean isTokenTypeSupported(JsonWebToken parsedToken) {
    String type = parsedToken.getType();
    return Objects.isNull(type) || SUPPORTED_TOKEN_TYPES.contains(type);
}
```

and in `KeycloakOIDCIdentityProvider` 
```
protected boolean isTokenTypeSupported(JsonWebToken parsedToken) {
    String type = parsedToken.getType();
    if (Objects.isNull(type)) {
        return false;
    }
    return super.isTokenTypeSupported(parsedToken);
}
```

### Version

25.0.6

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Access token can be validated in case it doesn't contain `typ` claim.

### Actual behavior

External tokens that do not contain `typ` claim and user info check disabled cannot be exchanged to internal because of error `invalid_token`

### How to Reproduce?

Configure as `OpenID Connect v1.0` identity provider that doesn't set `typ` claim (e.g. Microsoft), disable user info check, and try to exchange external to internal token with grant_type = `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange` and subject_token_type=`urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:jwt`.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: ID token from refresh_token flow does not contain nonce even when using Nonce backwards compatible mapper
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Recently, we have migrated from Keycloak 20.0.5 to version 25.0.4 which broke an integration with a client that expects the `nonce` claim to be present in all ID tokens. However, after migrating to the new version, `nonce` is not being added to the ID token obtained using the `refresh_token` flow.

According to release notes, this is by design - https://www.keycloak.org/docs/25.0.0/upgrading/#nonce-claim-is-only-added-to-the-id-token.

The notes mention that for backwards compatibility purposes a mapper `Nonce backwards compatible` can be used to revert to old behaviour. This indeed causes the `nonce` claim to be included in access and refresh tokens, however, it is still not included in an ID token when using the `refresh_token` flow and therefore not achieving the actual old behaviour.

I successfully managed to reproduce this behaviour in the latest version 25.0.6 as well.

### Version

25.0.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When utilizing `refresh_token` flow for a client with `Nonce backwards compatible` mapper, the ID token should include `nonce` claim

### Actual behavior

ID token obtained using `refresh_token` flow does not contain `nonce` claim even when the client uses `Nonce backwards compatible` mapper

### How to Reproduce?

- Assign the `Nonce backwards compatible` mapper to a client's dedicated scope or some other scope the client uses
- Obtain token using `authorization_code` flow (even though this flow type probably does not matter) with `nonce` parameter specified when calling authorization endpoint
- Use refresh token to get new tokens using `refresh_token` flow
- Access and refresh tokens contain the original `nonce`, ID token does not

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Organization data is cached for each user even if realm never enabled organizations
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

This is a follow-up issue on discussion here: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/33403#discussion_r1781216978

Current version of organization caching is caching list of organization ids for users to remember what organization the user is member of.

The problem with this is, that we are querying all organizations for user with each user querying (both single result and list) because it is part of this method: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/model/storage-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/UserStorageManager.java#L119

Note, we are doing this on all codepaths querying users regardless of any other settings, only way to get rid of this is disable organization feature.

This results in the following:
1. realms cache contains one entry per user to remember all organizations for the user, there is a related issue to this: #33412
2. Even if we move the entries to user cache as suggested in #33412 we would essentially halve the user cache, because each user will have 2 entries instead of one (CachedUser data and CachedOrganizationIds).
3. For realms that do not use organizations (and never did) this will result in the `realms` cache with a lot of entries `(realm_id, empty_list)`. Note this will be true for every realm and every deployment migrated to Keycloak 26. I suspect for realms with a lot of users this will mean the `realms` cache is basically useless for realms/clients/groups/roles etc. because these entities will be pushed out by many `CachedOrganizationIds` entries.

To me 3. is important especially since this impacts everyone upgrading to Keycloak 26.

 

### Version

main

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If realm does not use organizations (and no organizations exist) do not query and cache anything related to organizations.

### Actual behavior

We are storing things related to orgs regardless of settings

### How to Reproduce?

Login to any realm and observe this key: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/412f1f85a927f1f234e8562d9033d3786b79db29/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/cache/infinispan/organization/InfinispanOrganizationProvider.java#L346 is cached

### Anything else?

The authentication flow containing some organization conditional checks is unrelated to this issue as this is happening all the time users are queried not only when the organization related steps are done as part of authentication.

To workaround this issue it is possible to disable organization feature. However, this is not suitable for mixed deployments where some realms user orgs and some not.

Closes #33424

issue_title: 404 in admin console when unlinking managed user from organizations
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When some user is linked to the organization as "managed" member (so for example he was added to the realm by authentication to the identity provider, which is linked with the particular organization), then there is 404 displayed when trying to unlink such user from the organization.

AFAIK unlinking the user from the organization should remove the user and that is done under the covers. But it seems that admin console tries to redirect back to such user, which don't work as user does not exists anymore.


### Version

nightly (from 2024-10-01)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

User is unlinked and deleted (that is done), but then some sensible page is displayed to the administrator. Probably the generic "Users" page.

### Actual behavior

There is 404 error in the admin console

### How to Reproduce?

- Follow the steps similar to the organization blog https://www.keycloak.org/2024/06/announcement-keycloak-organizations.html . Some details:
-- Setup realm `orgdemo` and add organization `orga` with the domain `orga.com` to it.
-- Setup realm `orga` with the client and with user with email `jdoe@orga.com`
-- Setup identity provider in the realm `orgdemo` and link this IDP with the `orga` organization
-- Trying to login as user in the `orgdemo` with username like `jdoe@orga.com` . This will redirect user to the login page of `orga` realm. After login as `jdoe`, the user is redirected back to the `orgdemo` realm. At this point, user `jdoe@orga.com` is registered in the realm `orgdemo` as managed member of the organization `orga` .

- Now go to the admin console and go to the realm `orgdemo` and user `jdoe` and tab `Organizations` and click to "remove"  to remove the user from the organization

![Screenshot from 2024-10-01 13-44-03](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dfa463b9-2638-4d71-89fd-38ca3eb06a6b)

- Now the user is unlinked and removed, but there is 404

![Screenshot from 2024-10-01 13-44-46](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ef834e9c-92b0-4d13-9128-c852098d45c0)



### Anything else?

When removing the unmanaged user from the organization (For example the user, which was manually linked to the organization by administrator), the unlinking from organization works as expected.

issue_title: Avoid caching `RealmModel` in `CachedOrganization`
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

A `RealmModel` is created per Keycloak transaction and it is associated with the transaction, making it unusable. Also, it is a large object graph taking almost 30MB per instance.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/a0ad680346934980adb33c320352d0b29a007d77/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/cache/infinispan/organization/CachedOrganization.java#L35

We observed a performance penalty, see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/33331, due to the same reason. I believe the number of organizations won't be as large as the number of users but it can be a problem in the future. 

Other models are also cached but I'm not sure how large they can be

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/5218af1e79a8e2e88d732d26fc88c3c363ff1208/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/cache/infinispan/organization/CachedOrganization.java#L41-L42

Please review.

issue_title: LDAP groups not showing members in Groups when using memberOf attribute
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

When using the group-ldap-mapper to sync LDAP groups using the `memberOf` attribute into Keycloak, the groups section doesn't show any member within.

Meanwhile the membership can only be seen on the user detail section, in the groups tab.

LDAP configuration for the user
```
objectclass: inetOrgPerson
objectclass: organizationalPerson
objectclass: person
objectclass: top
cn: Bruce
sn: Wilson
mail: bwilson@keycloak.org
memberOf: cn=ldap-admin,ou=RealmRoles,dc=keycloak,dc=org
street: Elm 5
uid: bwilson
```

LDAP configuration for the group
```
objectclass: groupOfNames
objectclass: top
cn: ldap-admin
```


### Version

25.0.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Group membership should be displayed correctly both via `Users -> select user -> Groups` and via `Groups -> select group -> Members` 

### Actual behavior

Group membership is only visible when going through the user details screen. Checking the members of a group doesn't work.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a new group (e.g. "customers")
2. Create a User Federation
3. Create a group-ldap-mapper with the following settings:
- Mode: LDAP_ONLY
- User Groups Retrieve Strategy: GET_GROUPS_FROM_USER_MEMBEROF_ATTRIBUTE
- Member-Of LDAP Attribute: memberOf
- Groups Path: /customers
4. Sync all users
5. Check user membership (Users > {user} > Groups tab)
- User has the group assigned correctly 
6. Check groups members (Groups > customers > {group} > Members tab)
- No user is displayed in this section 

### Anything else?

As of now, I've discovered two workarounds for this issue (choose only one):

1. Change the mode from LDAP_ONLY to IMPORT, this will display the users correctly in the Groups section.

or 

2. Add the corresponding member entry in the LDAP group like the following:
```
objectclass: groupOfNames
objectclass: top
cn: ldap-admin
member: uid=bwilson,ou=People,dc=keycloak,dc=org
```
However, the GET_GROUPS_FROM_USER_MEMBEROF_ATTRIBUTE strategy should only look in the group attribute within the user record.
commit_message:
Ensure LDAPStorageMapper.getGroupMembers is taking the fetch strategy in consideration when retrieving the members
- fixes issue when MEMBER-OF strategy is selected but ignored when listing members

Closes #33477

issue_title: Can get authorization code on a non verified user with some specific kc_action (AIA)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

With a non verified user, using authorization code flow with some AIA (`kc_action=UPDATE_EMAIL` or `kc_action=VERIFY_EMAIL`), you can bypass the need to verify user's email and get redirected with a valid authorization code, allowing then to get valid tokens.

### Version

25.0.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

You need to verify the user before getting an authorization code, even if you add AIA actions such as `UPDATE_EMAIL` or `VERIFY_EMAIL`

### Actual behavior

When starting the authorization code flow with a non verified user to get an access token, if you add `kc_action=UPDATE_EMAIL`, you will bypass the need of verifying user's email first.

### How to Reproduce?

**Configuration**
- Realm:
  - Email on username : on
  - Login with email : on
  - Verify email : on
- One client with authorization flow : standard flow checked
- One user, with non verified email
- Preview feature `update_email` enabled

1. Call `http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/auth?response_type=code&client_id=my-oidc-client&redirect_uri=https://example.com&kc_action=UPDATE_EMAIL`
2. Login with the non verified user

You will be redirected to the redirect uri, with a valid authorization code, allowing then to get valid tokens : `https://example.com/?session_state=xx&iss=xx&code=73cb43af-2288-4564-9d36-43e4396ff9c6.7290759b-1019-4d08-b58c-51f6f9de9350.aeeeead8-f73d-4a92-a57b-d3b5efea8444`


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Return next action if the current action is not supported in AIA
Closes #33513

issue_title: Issue when running tests from IDE on embedded undertow ( org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Builder.setKeepAliveTime(java.time.Duration) )
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

When running the tests from the IDE with embedded undertow, it can be in some cases seen the error like this. For example `RPInitiatedLogoutTest.logoutWithExpiredSession` :

```
12:09:56,004 DEBUG [org.keycloak.testsuite.oauth.RPInitiatedLogoutTest] Set time offset, response {currentTime=1727960194, offset=9999}
12:09:56,012 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] Uncaught server error
java.lang.NoSuchMethodError: 'org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Builder org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Builder.setKeepAliveTime(java.time.Duration)'
	at org.infinispan.factories.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutorFactory.lambda$createExecutor$1(EnhancedQueueExecutorFactory.java:43)
	at java.base/java.util.Optional.orElseGet(Optional.java:364)
	at org.infinispan.factories.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutorFactory.createExecutor(EnhancedQueueExecutorFactory.java:36)
	at org.infinispan.executors.LazyInitializingBlockingTaskAwareExecutorService.initIfNeeded(LazyInitializingBlockingTaskAwareExecutorService.java:206)
	at org.infinispan.executors.LazyInitializingBlockingTaskAwareExecutorService.execute(LazyInitializingBlockingTaskAwareExecutorService.java:193)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture.asyncSupplyStage(CompletableFuture.java:1782)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture.supplyAsync(CompletableFuture.java:2005)
	at org.infinispan.util.concurrent.BlockingManagerImpl.supplyBlockingOperation(BlockingManagerImpl.java:163)
	at org.infinispan.util.concurrent.BlockingManagerImpl.supplyBlocking(BlockingManagerImpl.java:141)
	at org.infinispan.expiration.impl.ExpirationManagerImpl.entryExpiredInMemory(ExpirationManagerImpl.java:145)
	at org.infinispan.container.impl.AbstractInternalDataContainer.get(AbstractInternalDataContainer.java:94)
	at org.infinispan.container.impl.AbstractInternalDataContainer.get(AbstractInternalDataContainer.java:105)
	at org.infinispan.cache.impl.SimpleCacheImpl.get(SimpleCacheImpl.java:558)
	at org.infinispan.cache.impl.StatsCollectingCache.get(StatsCollectingCache.java:49)
	at org.infinispan.cache.impl.EncoderCache.get(EncoderCache.java:735)
	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.changes.UserSessionPersistentChangelogBasedTransaction.get(UserSessionPersistentChangelogBasedTransaction.java:61)
	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.PersistentUserSessionProvider.getUserSession(PersistentUserSessionProvider.java:286)
	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.PersistentUserSessionProvider.getUserSession(PersistentUserSessionProvider.java:282)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.AuthenticationManager.verifyIdentityToken(AuthenticationManager.java:1493)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.AuthenticationManager.authenticateIdentityCookie(AuthenticationManager.java:862)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.logout(LogoutEndpoint.java:259)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:77)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43)
	at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:569)
```

The issue is because infinispan expects jboss-threads 3.6.1 (which Keycloak on Quarkus distribution has), but the testsuite uses jboss-threads 2.4.0

### Version

nightly (from 2024-10-03)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

It is possible to run any tests from IDE with Keycloak on embedded undertow and it works

### Actual behavior

Some tests are failing due the issue above.

### How to Reproduce?

Run `RPInitiatedLogoutTest.logoutWithExpiredSession` . It happens probably in the tests, which rely on infinispan expiring some outdated entities, which means the tests relying on `InfinispanTestTimeServiceRule` . But not sure exactly for all the tests when it happens...

### Anything else?

_No response_
